Official-source watchlist

Regulatory Watch 2026

A concise, dated view of the EU AI Act milestones that affect practical governance work in 2026 and beyond. Each point links to the official Commission or EUR-Lex source used for the review.

Use the Assessment Path Open Commission overview

Reviewed 11 July 2026

What is active, arriving, or still on the timeline

Dates below are a working governance reference. The Commission guidance and the Regulation remain the authoritative sources, and implementation guidance can change.

Active since 2 February 2025

Article 4: AI literacy

Providers and deployers must take measures to ensure a sufficient level of AI literacy for staff and other people operating AI systems on their behalf.

Read Article 4

Active since 2 August 2025

GPAI obligations

General-purpose AI model obligations and governance milestones have applied on the Commission timeline since August 2025, with specific duties depending on the actor and model.

Check current status

From 2 August 2026

Article 50: transparency

Providers and deployers of certain AI systems face transparency duties, including information and marking expectations for specified AI-generated or manipulated content.

Read the FAQ

Current Commission guidance

High-risk standalone systems

The Commission's current high-risk guidance describes the implementation timeline for standalone Annex III systems as 2 December 2027 after the political agreement on the Digital Omnibus.

Read high-risk guidance

Current Commission guidance

Product-embedded high-risk systems

The current guidance describes 2 August 2028 for high-risk AI systems embedded in regulated products, subject to the legal and implementation context described by the official sources.

Follow standardisation

Always check the use case

Risk is contextual

Classification depends on the system, purpose, actor, deployment context, affected people, and applicable sector obligations. A label alone is not an assessment.

Open Risk Tiering
Important: This page is not legal advice. The review date is 11 July 2026. Confirm the current Regulation, Commission guidance, national implementation measures, standards, and sector rules before relying on a date or obligation.

From watch item to evidence

What this changes in PALO

The watchlist is useful only when it changes what a team records, reviews, and monitors. These are the practical PALO responses for a working file.

Watch pointPALO responseEvidence to keep
AI literacyIdentify people who operate, supervise, procure, or review the system and route them to role-specific literacy actions.Training record, role map, escalation contact, review date.
Article 50 transparencyMap where users or affected people need information, content marking, disclosure, or an explanation of the system's role.Notice copy, content provenance decision, UI or workflow evidence, owner.
High-risk timelineUse Risk Tiering early, then preserve the rationale and open questions instead of waiting for a final implementation date.Classification rationale, affected-person analysis, control plan, source snapshot.
Article 27 contextRoute public-authority, public-service, credit, and insurance deployment contexts to a contextual FRIA check.FRIA scope decision, rights impact notes, mitigation and consultation record.
Changing guidanceAssign an owner and review trigger so the governance record is revisited when official guidance or the system changes.Watch log, source links, version, decision log, next review date.

Classify

Risk Tiering

Start with purpose, actor, use, and affected people.

Open the calculator

Assess

Contextual FRIA

Keep the Article 27 scope decision and rights impact evidence together.

Open the FRIA

Evidence

Assessment Path

Route the work and export a versioned local evidence bundle.

Build the bundle

Agents

PALO-AM

Extend the assessment to delegated action, authority, and autonomy.

Open PALO-AM

Official sources

Use the following sources to verify dates and obligations before a governance decision is made.

  • European Commission: regulatory framework for AI
  • European Commission: guidelines for high-risk AI systems
  • European Commission: transparency and AI-generated content FAQ
  • European Commission: AI Act standardisation
  • EUR-Lex: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689
Complementary operational watch. For public changes in corporate privacy policies and terms of service, see PolicyWatcher Observatory and its policy timeline. This is an external monitoring surface, not an official source for AI Act obligations.

Copyright 2026 PALO Framework. Verify current sources before relying on a regulatory conclusion.
